properties.remark: CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 1 WNW TUSTIN. OCFA REPORTS 2 VEHICLES WERE STUCK IN WATER ON THE 4TH STREET OFF RAMP ON SR55 NORTHBOUND. OCCUPANTS WERE STUCK INSIDE AND ON TOP OF THEIR VEHICLES AND REQUIRED RESCUING. WATER WAS UP TO THE WINDSHIELD OF THE 2 VEHICLES IN VIDEO POSTED BY OCFA PIO. RAINFALL RATES 0.5 TO 0.7 INCH IN THE PAST HOUR 10 TO 11 PM. 0.76 AT HICKS CANYON ALERT WHICH IS NEAR HIGHWAY 261 EAST OF TUSTIN.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 1 N OCEANSIDE. FLASH FLOODING ON SR78 AT COLLEGE AVENUE REPORTED BY CALTRANS. FULL HIGHWAY CLOSURE WITH DISABLED VEHICLES DUE TO WATER UP TO 2.5 FEET DEEP. RAINFALL RATE 0.84 INCHES AT CRQ CARLSBAD AIRPORT MIDNIGHT TO 1 AM.
REPORT OF A MUDSLIDE LEADING TO ROCKS AND DIRT BLOCKING EASTBOUND LANES ON VIA DE SANTA FE.
REPORT OF A MUDSLIDE ON OLD JULIAN HIGHWAY BY MM 4.5.

2022 FAIRVIEW BURN SCAR. RAINFALL RATE 0.5 TO 0.8 INCHES OBSERVED WITH PEAK 4 TO 5 PM. SHOWERS AND ISOLATED THUNDERSTORMS. TOTAL RAINFALL 1.30 INCHES JUST WEST OF CRANSTON STATION NEAR VALLE VISTA AT BLACKBURN ROAD AND STATE HIGHWAY 74. THE LOCATION HAD FLOODING AT 230 PM AS WELL.
GIANT 4X5 FOOT BOULDER HIGHWAY 78 AT WYNOLA ROAD.

MUD AND ROCKS COVERING ENTIRE UPBOUND LANE OF HIGHWAY 330. NEARBY RUNNING SPRINGS REPORTED 0.28 INCH OF RAIN IN THE PAST HOUR AND 0.83 INCH IN THE PAST 6 HOURS.
LARGE BOULDERS IN UPBOUND LANES OF HIGHWAY 330 NEAR THE 4000 FT LEVEL. RAINFALL RATES IN THIS AREA ARE AROUND 0.20 INCH PER HOUR.

BOULDER SIZE OF CAR IN HIGHWAY 38 LANES AT BARTON FLATS ROAD. THIS EAST OF EL DORADO BURN SCAR. REPORTING STATIONS INDICATE 0.75 INCHES RAINFALL IN PAST 24 HOURS.
MUD SLIDE AT SIERRA AVE AND LYTLE CREEK RD PARTIALLY BLOCKING LANES. THIS AREA HAS HAD 2 INCHES OF RAIN TODAY.
SEVERAL ROCKS BLOCKING BOTH LANES OF HIGHWAY 38. BARELY ENOUGH ROOM FOR ONE VEHICLE TO PASS. ONE AND HALF INCHES OF RAIN HAD FALLEN IN THE AREA IN THE AFTERNOON.
LARGE BOULDER IN LANE ON HIGHWAY 138. MORMON ROCK RAWS WITH 1.7 RAINFALL AND PEAK RATE 0.3 PER HOUR AT 9 PM. I-15 INTERSECTION.

VEHICLE STUCK IN THE MUD ON HIGHWAY 74 AT BLACKBURN RD, JUST OUTSIDE OF THE FAIRVIEW FIRE BURN SCAR. STORM TOTAL PRECIPITATION IN THIS AREA WAS AROUND 0.50 INCH, ABOUT HALF OF WHICH FELL BETWEEN 1 AND 2 PM.
SEVERAL ROCKS ON ROADWAY. 10000 DEL DIOS HWY FOLLOWING HEAVY RAIN SHOWERS.
SEVERAL ROCKS BLOCKING THE WESTBOUND LANE OF DEL DIOS HIGHWAY NEAR MILE MARKER 10.
LARGE TREE, SOIL, MUD AND ROCKS SLID DOWN ON HIGHWAY 243, ABOUT 150 CUBIC YARDS. PHOTOS SHARED. RAINFALL 2 INCHES PAST 24 HOURS.
ROCK SLIDE IN WESTBOUND LANE OF HIGHWAY 74 NEAR HOT SPRINGS CANYON ROAD. OVER 1 INCH OF RAIN HAS FALLEN IN THIS LOCATION SINCE 10 AM.
BOULDERS IN BOTH LANES OF HIGHWAY 38 JUST WEST OF ANGELUS OAKS.
LARGE BOULDER THE SIZE OF A TOILET ALONG WITH OTHER ROCKS IN THE WESTBOUND LANE OF DEL DIOS HIGHWAY NEAR RANCHO RD.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 3 SE SAN MARCOS. HARMONY GROVE ROAD FLOODED AT WILGEN DRIVE NEAR ELFIN FOREST WITH HIGH FLOW WATER. 4 FEET OF WATER FLOWING BOTH DIRECTIONS INTO ELFIN PARK. 1.33 INCHES RAIN IN SAN MARCOS WITH HOURLY RATES OF 0.35 INCHES SINCE 3 PM.

SIG ALERT ISSUED BY CHP. HIGHWAY 78 AT EL CAMINO REAL. 0.68 INCHES FROM 4 TO 5 PM AND 1.06 INCHES AT FW9462 FROM 3 TO 6 PM. 0.96 AT CRQ AIRPORT. 1.35 INCHES FROM NOON TO 6 PM. BUENA VISTA CREEK RUNS THIS AREA. WESTBOUND HIGHWAY 78 CLOSED THROUGH 930 PM.

ROCKSLIDE COVERING ALL LANES AT THE INTERSECTION OF LAKE WOHLFORD RD AND OAKVALE RD EAST OF ESCONDIDO. AREA HAS RECEIVED 2.5 INCHES OF RAIN IN THE PAST 24 HOURS.

LARGE BOULDERS BLOCKING BOTH THE EAST AND WEST BOUND LANES OF SR 94 AND SR 188 AT THE INTERSECTION OF CAMPO RD AND TECATE RD NEAR PORTERO (WEST OF CAMPO). AREA HAS RECEIVED 1.5 TO 2 INCHES OF RAIN IN PAST 24 HOURS.
3 TO 4 FT ROCKS BLOCKING RD AT THE INTERSECTION OF LYONS VALLEY RD AND JAPATUL RD IN EASTERN SAN DIEGO COUNTY. AREA HAS RECEIVED 2.5 TO 3 INCHES OF RAIN IN PAST 24 HOURS.

REPORTS OF CARS STUCK IN MUD FLOW THAT IS BLOCKING LANES AND ROAD WASHED OUT AT THE INTERSECTION OF ANZA RD AND LINDA ROSEA RD NEAR TEMECULA, CA. AREA HAS RECEIVED 1 TO 1.5 INCHES OF RAIN IN PAST 24 HOURS.

MUD AND LARGE BOULDERS IN EAST BOUND LANES AT 15805 EL MONTE RD WHICH IS IN BETWEEN SANTEE AND ALPINE. AREA HAS RECEIVED AROUND 2 INCHES OF RAIN IN THE PAST 24 HOURS.

ROAD CLOSED PALOMAR MOUNTAIN SOUTH GRADE RD FROM THE 44.8 MILE MARKER TO THE 45 MILE MARKER DUE TO A ROCK AND MUD SLIDE. AREA HAS RECEIVED 2 TO 4 INCHES OF RAIN IN THE PAST 12 HOURS AND 7 TO 10 INCHES IN PAST 48 HOURS.
3 FOOT BY 3 FOOT BOULDERS IN EAST BOUND LANES ON SR 74 (ORTEGA HWY) AND CANDY STORE, NEAR BEAR CANYON LOOP TRAIL. AREA HAS RECEIVED ABOUT 1.5 INCHES OF RAIN IN PAST 24 HOURS.
BOULDERS SCATTERED IN ROAD FOR 3 MILES GOING NORTH BOUND ON SR79 AND CHIHUAHUA VALLEY RD EAST OF PALOMAR MTN. AREA HAS RECEIVED 1 TO 1.5 INCHES OF RAIN IN PAST 24 HOURS.

SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY FIRE REPORTS SWIFT WATER RESCUE OF ONE OCCUPANT FROM A TRUCK. VIDEO SHOWS RUSHING WATER ABOUT HALF WAY UP THE TRUCK. OCCUPANT WAS RESCUED AND BROUGHT TO SAFETY AND ASSESSED FOR INJURIES.
DEBRIS ON ROADWAY ON SILVERADO CANYON RD AT OLIVE. 0.71 INCHES PAST 3 HOURS.

PHOTO OF LARGE TREE LANDING ON DESCANSO ELEMENTARY SCHOOL BUILDING SHARED BY SAN DIEGO OFFICE OF EDUCATION. WEST WINDS GUSTED TO 44 MPH ON RAWS AT 5 AM AND 11 AM. MEDIA REPORT ON ABC10 SAN DIEGO. SCHOOL WAS CLOSED AT THE TIME, NO INJURIES.
1-INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN. SHINGLES BLOWN OFF.

LINE OF HEAVY SHOWERS MOVING THROUGH ORANGE COUNTY. LARGE MATURE PINE TREE KNOCKED DOWN NEAR INTERSECTION OF MACARTHUR AND MAIN STREET. DIAMETER OF TREE ESTIMATED 3 FEET, WITH LOTS OF DEBRIS AND BRANCHES ALSO DOWN.

MESONET STATION XIMP IMPERIAL BEACH PIER.

LANDSLIDE IN SAN CLEMENTE. A CLIFF NEAR THE OCEAN COLLAPSED. FOUR APARTMENT STRUCTURES IN THE 1500 BLOCK OF BUENA VISTA HAVE BEEN EVACUATED. BUENA VISTA FROM AVENIDA FLORENCIO TO CALLE COLINA HAVE BEEN CLOSED. NO INJURIES REPORTED.
MESONET STATION XZUN SILVER STRAND BEACH - SQUALL LINE OF HEAVY RAIN.

LARGE TREE DOWNED, RESULTING IN A POWER OUTAGE.
SR 18 WATERMAN CANYON CLOSED DUE TO MULTIPLE ROCK AND MUD SLIDES. 1 TO 2 INCHES RAINFALL IN THIS REGION SO FAR.
CALTRANS REPORTS LANDSLIDE MUD AND DEBRIS COVERING THE ROAD SR 94 AND BARRETT SMITH RD. 1.3 RAINFALL TODAY.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS FAST MOVING WATER OVER CLOSED ROAD VIA MONTEZUMA AT MURRIETA CREEK.
LANDSLIDE HAS CLOSED BANNER GRADE HIGHWAY 78. 3 INCHES OF RAIN AT JULIAN PAST 2 DAYS. SIG ALERT ISSUED BY CALTRANS CHP.

SEVERAL SECTIONS OF MOUNTAIN VIEW ROAD WERE COVERED IN MUD AS MUCH AS 2 FEET DEEP WITH DAMAGE ON THE SIDE PAVEMENT. EL MIRAGE RAWS RECORDED 1.23 INCHES OF RAIN BETWEEN 430 AND 530 PM MAY 21. RADAR TOTAL ESTIMATES OF 2 TO 4 INCHES SOUTHWEST AND JUST NORTH OF THIS AREA. THUNDERSTORMS ALSO OCCURRED MAY 20. STANDING WATER ALONG EL MIRAGE ROAD INCLUDING ONE HOME SURROUNDED BY WATER. THIS AREA IS A BLM OFF ROAD RECREATION AND DRY LAKE BED. THE LAKE WAS DRY PRIOR TO THE RAIN AND NOW HAS PARTIAL WATER.
Thermal ASOS reported 1/2 mi visibility in blowing dust between 250 am and 3 am. Visibility was 1 mi or less from approximate 245 am to 345 am.
Borrego Springs AWOS reported 1/2 mi visibility in blowing dust from thunderstorm outflow between 315 and 335 am.
Palm Springs ASOS reported 1.5 mi visibility in blowing dust from thunderstorm outflow between 330 and 415 am. Visibility briefly dropped to 1.25 mi at 405 am.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 SW Coachella. Report of palm tree in the road near the intersection of Calhoun St and 54th Ave in Thermal. Following reports confirm this was due to a car accident and not weather.
Report of palm tree in the road near the intersection of Calhoun St and 54th Ave in Thermal. Thermal Airport reported wind gusts of 40 to 50 mph between 230 am and 4 am.
Report from mPING: 1-inch tree limbs broken and shingles blown off near Anza. Nearby obs showed wind gusts of 25-30 mph at the time.
Semi trailer overturned by wind. Photo shared. Thunderstorm moved through deserts 530 to 700 pm. Nearby sites reported 35 mph further west in Borrego Springs.
Medium sized boulder on Highway 74 just east of Pinyon Pines.
Rock slide on Highway 38 blocking entire lane. Cars crossing double yellow to get by.
Water with debris, rocks and mud on Old Highway 80 at In Ko Pah Park Road.
SR78 / BANNER DR, HEAVY MUD BLOCKING SB LANES.
SR18 RIM OF THE WORLD HIGHWAY LRG BOULDER IN LANES.
Report from mPING: Street/road flooding on Gene Autry Trail. Vehicles stranded.
ROCK DEBRIS IN ALL LANES.
Video at Sheep Creek with boulders, rocks and high water crossing state highway 2. 6.23 of rain in one dat reported at Wrightwood. Time estimated.
Box Canyon Rd at Painted Canyon Rd flooded. Caller was stuck on the flooded road. Rescued by CalFire.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF ROADWAY FLOODING ON THE I10 IN CABAZON.
Highway 78 washed out at Yaqui Pass.
Report from mPING: Street/road flooding; Street/road closed; Vehicles stranded.
SR18 AND LAKE GREGORY ROCK ALL OVER ROADWAY.
Large boulders on Highway 79 at Royal Road. Near Fire Station 56.

Video of debris flow in Valley of the Falls. Time estimated. Upper elevations Raywood Flats reached 11 inches of rain in one day. El Dorado burn scar area. Oak Glen had 0.91 inches in 1 hour rainfall. Yucaipa Ridge 0.75 in 1 hour.
Two vehicles stuck in rushing water on Dillon Road and Landfill Road.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 1 E Wrightwood. State highway 2 is closed at Sheep Creek drainage. Massive boulders and water flow was captured on video by public works while trying to clear the highway during the TC Hilary storm. Estimated time based on videos. 6 inches of rain occurred in Wrightwood in 18 hours.

Previous report was 1 bridge but now 2 bridges are reported washed out on Seven Oaks Road to Glass Road. There are 5 to 15 persons cut off from access. There is ongoing search and rescue for missing person possibly washed into Barton Creek or Santa Ana River. This is north of state highway 38 Rim of the World. This area had 5 to 7 inches on Sunday in about 15 hours.
Video in Oak Glen Potato canyon off mud and rocks across roadway. Oak Glen reported 0.91 in one hour. El Dorado fire scar from 2020. Estimated time.
SR74 & Ritter Ave. HEAVY FLOODING WITH NUMEROUS VEHICLES STRANDED.
Significant flooding of streets in San Jacinto.

State highway 38 is washed out (half of road to center line) at Heart Bar along Santa Ana river. Highway is closed Jenks Lake Rd to Lakewood Rd until contracted repairs to rebuild road can be completed. This road goes to Big Bear City. Heart Bar received 7 inches of rain on Sunday in less than 12 hours with peak rates around 1 inch per hour. Caltrans shared photos. This is north and east of El Dorado fire scar. Estimated time of wash out. Other areas along the riverbank are eroded with damage to roadway.
Gavilan Rd & Multiview Dr Roadway Flooding. FULL ROAD CLOSURE.
Valley Of The Falls Dr & Summit Dr Roadway is washed out.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 4 SSE Big Bear Lake. 30 residents in Seven Oaks residents cut off from rescue when the Santa Ana River overflowed and Glass Road was damaged. One person may be missing. Estimated time. Seven oaks road is closed for emergency crews.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 7 NE Mt San Jacinto. Whitewater river received locally 5 inches of rain and up to 13 inches at Mission in upper headwaters. A massive flood with rocks and debris went down the river and under I-10, splashing mud on I-10 overpass and causing closure and pushing debris on highway 111 overpass. Both Union Pacific rail tracks were undercut when the river broke a diversion bern at the California and Colorado river aqueduct and broad massive flooding downstream to Palm Springs. The water debris appeared to be 10 to 15 feet high and buckled/cracked the bridge footing at White Water and severed fiber cables causing outages. 50 to 100 trains enter the Coachella Valley each day and traffic is all stopped. Video of late afternoon boulders in the initial flood waters. Whitewater river gauge failed at 16 feet maximum. USGS gauges showed 3 to 5 pm peak higher terrain and 7 to 11 pm peak in larger drainages from upper elevations.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 5 SSW Desert Hot Springs. Indian Canyon (and Date Palm) closed UFN. Massive 250 yards of mud and debris in the Whitewater river crossed and undercut the concrete overflow on the 4 lane road. Water was still flowing and a field of boulders evident on the road. Socal edison power poles were snapped along Indian Canyon road and also along I-10. Half of the road, 2 lanes, were washed away near the Union Pacific Railroad tracks. Flash flood occurred Sunday night in waves of high water, mud and debris. Logs were pushed against powerpoles and bank erosion shows the water was up to 6 feet deep.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 3 NE Palm Springs. I-10 at Date Palm was closed in both directions (8 lanes) due to extensive mud and water that crossed the interstate Sunday night. Both directions were down to 1 lane and miles of traffic backed up as of 5 pm Monday. Ramon and Salvia washes crossed all lanes with mud and water. Caltrans continues to remove mud. Estimated time of onset. Both train tracks at Date Palm underpass covered in over a mile of mud and one train derailed with clean up efforts beginning. Whitewater river gauge failed at maximum 16 feet.

I-10 at Date Palm was closed in both directions (8 lanes) due to extensive mud and water that crossed the interstate Sunday night. Both directions were down to 1 lane and miles of traffic backed up as of 5 pm Monday. Ramon and Salvia washes crossed all lanes with mud and water. Caltrans continues to remove mud. Estimated time of onset. Both train tracks at Date Palm underpass covered in over a mile of mud and one train derailed with clean up efforts beginning.

Indian Canyon (and Date Palm) closed UFN. Massive 250 yards of mud and debris in the Whitewater river crossed and undercut the concrete overflow on the 4 lane road. Water was still flowing and a field of boulders evident on the road. Socal edison power poles were snapped along Indian Canyon road and also along I-10. Half of the road, 2 lanes, were washed away near the Union Pacific Railroad tracks. Flash flood occurred Sunday night in waves of high water, mud and debris. Logs were pushed against powerpoles and bank erosion shows the water was up to 6 feet deep.

State highway 2 is closed at Sheep Creek drainage. Massive boulders and water flow was captured on video by public works while trying to clear the highway during the TC Hilary storm. Estimated time based on videos. 6 inches of rain occurred in Wrightwood in 18 hours.
Mias Canyon bridge reported washed out this is Apple burn scar area.

30 residents in Seven Oaks residents cut off from rescue when the Santa Ana River overflowed and Glass Road was damaged. One person may be missing. Estimated time. Seven oaks road is closed for emergency crews.
Large trees down and large branches along Santa Ana river and Riverview Golf Course.

Whitewater river received locally 5 inches of rain and up to 13 inches at Mission in upper headwaters. A massive flood with rocks and debris went down the river and under I-10, splashing mud on I-10 overpass and causing closure and pushing debris on highway 111 overpass. Both Union Pacific rail tracks were undercut when the river broke a diversion bern at the California and Colorado river aqueduct and broad massive flooding downstream to Palm Springs. The water debris appeared to be 10 to 15 feet high and buckled/cracked the bridge footing at White Water and severed fiber cables causing outages. 50 to 100 trains enter the Coachella Valley each day and traffic is all stopped. Video of late afternoon boulders in the initial flood waters.

****Delayed report. Avenue 52 and Education Way. CalFire Riverside unit video. Several vehicles stranded in 1 to 2 feet of water. One vehicle had an occupant that was rescued. 0.86 inch in 1 hour at La Quinta between 1 and 2 pm. 0.72 in 1 hour at Thermal South between 3 and 4 pm and 1.47 in 3 hours. Oasis in Torres Martinez reservation with 2.27 inches and La Quinta 2.58 inches between 1 and 7 pm.
Visibility estimated as low as 100 feet locally along Interstate 10 north of Palm Springs.

This area still recovering from TC Hilary flooding. 79650 Varner Road flooded and closed on Hammond from 68th to Grand. Thousand Palms reported east wind gust 45 mph and 1.18 rainfall. Location just north of I-10.
Lincoln Ave at 67959 flooded county road. 1.82 inches rainfall at Thermal South and 2 hours of 0.72 per hour in thunderstorms. Road still flooded at 1030 am September 2 per Caltrans.
Avenue 50 reported flooded by crews at Empire Polo Field. Coachella gauge recorded 1.89 rainfall in 4 hours. La Quinta station reported .94 in 1 hour and 2.59 total.
North Indian Canyon closed by Police due to blowing dust and low visibility. This was the area severely flooded and damaged in Whitewater wash on August 20.

Vista Chino road closed due to blowing dust. North Gene Autry was being monitored. This area severely flooded with mud and water on August 20th. PSP Palm Springs airport had northwest winds sustained at 15 mph and gusts to 28 mph since 3 pm with a peak around 8 pm.
Vista Chino Road at Gene Autry Trail closed due to low visibility from blowing sand in the Whitewater River wash. Upstream winds through the San Gorgonio Pass gusted 30-50 mph.
Wind gusts of 38 to 53 mph on weather stations in Alpine. Patio furniture throw around and damaged. This was associated with initial thunderstorms and dry subcloud layer.
Scripps La Jolla Buoy.
Zuniga Jetty (WXFLOW).
Mission Bay North Jetty (WXFLOW).
Imperial Beach Pier (WXFLOW).

Thunderstorm microburst southwest (shift from the east) wind gust squall to 55 mph recorded at ASOS KCRQ Carlsbad Palomar airport at 1130 pm December 21. Survey found a dozen trees downed including a uprooted 3 foot wide eucalyptus, uprooted 2 foot wide pine and snapped 18 inch pepper tree. Many large branches and tops of trees were snapped off. Tree damage on both north and south sides of the CRQ airport which is on Palomar road, and most trees laying to the east. Yarrow, Kellogg, El Camino, Faraday, Camino Vida Roble and Corte Del Nogal roads affected. This area is commercial businesses such as K1 Speed and Calloway Golf HQ and 4 hotels. Airport reported a sign damaged near the tower.
